wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the FactChecking Live project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a real-time fact-checking system that captures live audio, processes it through speech recognition, extracts verifiable claims, retrieves evidence from multiple sources, and synthesizes factual verdicts. Document the key features including real-time audio capture modes, AI-powered claim extraction, multi-source evidence retrieval, Live Activity integration with Dynamic Island, and Siri Shortcuts support. Describe the target audience, use cases, and the overall value proposition. Include architectural highlights, technology stack overview, and system capabilities. Provide both conceptual understanding for beginners and technical context for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dynamic Island Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Dynamic Island implementation using ActivityKit. Detail the Live Activity attributes definition including captureMode, sourceApp, and startedAt properties. Document the ContentState structure with status tracking, verdict information, confidence scores, and elapsed time management. Explain the VerificationStatus enumeration covering listening, transcribing, extracting, searching, verifying, and complete states. Cover the VerdictType enumeration for different fact-checking outcomes (TRUE, SUBSTANTIALLY TRUE, MISLEADING, FALSE, UNVERIFIABLE). Document the ActivityAttributes protocol implementation, content state management, and real-time status updates. Include configuration requirements, entitlements setup, and performance optimization for smooth Dynamic Island updates during fact-check sessions.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the architectural design patterns used in FactChecking Live. Detail the observer pattern implementation for state management through the AppState and @Observable properties. Explain the service locator pattern used for dependency injection with shared instances of core services. Document the pipeline pattern for sequential processing stages from audio capture through verdict synthesis. Include the MVVM-inspired architecture with clear separation between views, view models, and data models. Provide concrete examples from the codebase showing how these patterns are implemented, including the FactCheckCoordinator as the central orchestrator and the role of singleton patterns for service management. Address the benefits and trade-offs of each pattern choice.

wiki_catalogs.name: Siri Shortcuts Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Siri Shortcuts integration in FactChecking Live. Document the shortcut configuration process, including intent definition, parameter handling, and user workflow setup. Explain the StartFactCheckIntent and StopFactCheckIntent implementations, covering their execution patterns, state management, and user feedback mechanisms. Detail the FactShieldShortcuts utility functions and how they facilitate shortcut registration and intent handling. Include step-by-step setup instructions for configuring Siri Shortcuts, voice command patterns, and accessibility considerations. Document the integration with the main fact-checking pipeline, showing how shortcuts trigger session initiation and termination. Provide examples of custom voice commands, error handling scenarios, and troubleshooting common Siri Shortcuts issues. Address privacy considerations and user permission requirements for voice activation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audio Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the audio processing services that handle real-time audio capture and processing. Document the AudioCaptureService implementation including microphone and system audio capture modes, AVAudioEngine configuration, and audio session management. Explain the AudioBufferProcessor for real-time audio buffer management, format conversion, and quality optimization. Detail the AudioSessionManager for audio session lifecycle management, interruption handling, and device configuration. Include audio format specifications, buffer size optimization, latency considerations, and performance tuning. Document error handling strategies for audio interruptions, permission issues, and hardware conflicts. Provide practical examples of audio capture setup, buffer processing workflows, and integration with speech recognition services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the core entities in FactChecking Live. Detail the Claim model structure including claim text, confidence scores, timestamps, and extraction metadata. Document the Evidence model with supporting/correcting information, source attribution, relevance scores, and verification status. Explain the Verdict model containing final fact-checking results, confidence percentages, reasoning chains, and conclusion categories. Cover the FactCheckSession model for session lifecycle management, historical tracking, and result aggregation. Include field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business constraints. Document model relationships, inheritance hierarchies, and serialization formats. Provide examples of model instantiation, data transformation, and persistence patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Qwen API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the Qwen API integration used for AI-powered claim extraction and language model processing. Document the authentication configuration including API key setup and security best practices. Detail all available endpoints, request/response schemas, and payload formats for claim extraction and fact-checking operations. Include concrete examples of API requests with transcript data, expected response formats with confidence scores and reasoning chains. Document error handling strategies, rate limiting behavior, and retry mechanisms. Provide parameter specifications for different processing modes, model versions, and quality settings. Include performance optimization tips, timeout configurations, and monitoring approaches for API interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Home View
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the HomeView component, the primary landing page of FactChecking Live. Document the main layout structure including the hero card section with start button functionality, active session banner display, how-it-works educational section, and recent history preview. Explain the HeroCard component implementation with gradient styling, pulsing animation effects, and conditional state handling. Detail the ActiveSessionBanner component showing real-time session status, elapsed time tracking, and current claim display. Document the step-by-step workflow visualization in HowItWorksSection and the placeholder for recent history functionality. Include SwiftUI composition patterns, state management with @State variables, navigation integration, and toolbar configuration. Provide examples of component customization, accessibility features, and responsive design considerations for different screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for FactChecking Live. Include detailed prerequisites covering Xcode requirements, iOS version compatibility, and hardware requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, dependency installation via Swift Package Manager, and initial project setup. Document the build configuration process, entitlements setup, and signing requirements. Explain the initial configuration steps including API key setup for Qwen API integration. Include first-time user workflow from app launch to completing their first fact-check session. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and environment configuration problems. Make the content accessible to developers new to the project while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Widget Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for lock screen widget creation and Dynamic Island layout customization. Document the FactShieldWidget implementation including data binding, refresh logic, and content presentation. Explain the WidgetBundle configuration for widget registration and lifecycle management. Detail the widget's integration with the Live Activity system for real-time updates. Cover widget size variants, layout customization options, and user interaction patterns. Document data synchronization between widgets and the main application, including cache management and update frequency. Include examples of widget content formatting, status indicators, and progress tracking displays. Address widget performance optimization, memory management, and debugging techniques for widget-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the component relationships and interactions within the FactChecking Live architecture. Document the FactCheckCoordinator as the central orchestrator that manages the entire verification pipeline and coordinates between audio processing, speech recognition, claim extraction, evidence retrieval, and verdict synthesis services. Explain the data flow relationships between components and how they communicate through well-defined interfaces. Detail the role of AppState in maintaining global state and the coordination between UI components and backend services. Include sequence diagrams showing typical workflows from user initiation to final verdict delivery. Address the loose coupling achieved through protocol-based interfaces and dependency injection patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Broadcast Extension
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the ReplayKit broadcast extension implementation in FactChecking Live. Detail the SampleHandler class architecture, including broadcast session management, audio processing pipeline, and system audio capture mechanisms. Document the entitlements configuration in FactShieldBroadcast.entitlements and FactShield.entitlements, explaining the security model and app group communication requirements. Explain the inter-app communication patterns between the main app and broadcast extension, covering data sharing, state synchronization, and performance optimization strategies. Include setup instructions for enabling broadcast extension capabilities, configuring system audio permissions, and handling user consent flows. Document the audio processing pipeline within the broadcast extension, including buffer management, real-time processing constraints, and quality settings. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common broadcast extension issues, memory management considerations, and battery optimization techniques. Address security implications, sandbox restrictions, and compliance with Apple's guidelines for broadcast extensions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Speech Recognition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the speech recognition services that provide on-device transcription capabilities. Document the SpeechRecognitionService implementation including speech recognition setup, audio input configuration, and real-time transcription processing. Explain the TranscriptManager for rolling transcript management, partial result handling, and transcript aggregation. Detail the speech recognition pipeline including audio preprocessing, model inference, and result formatting. Document partial result handling for real-time feedback, confidence scoring, and transcript refinement. Include configuration options for recognition languages, acoustic models, and performance optimization. Provide practical examples of speech recognition setup, transcript processing workflows, and integration with claim extraction services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Enumerations and Constants
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for FactChecking Live's enumeration systems and constants. Document the AppTab enumeration for navigation tabs (home, history, settings) and their associated UI behaviors. Explain the AudioQuality enumeration with sample rate mappings and quality implications for audio processing. Detail the FactShieldError enumeration covering all error types, localized descriptions, and error handling strategies. Document the Source enumeration for evidence categorization and provenance tracking. Cover application-wide constants including API endpoints, configuration values, and system limits. Include usage examples, error propagation patterns, and best practices for working with enumerated types throughout the application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Search API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the web search API integration used for evidence retrieval and cross-verification. Document the search endpoint configuration, query construction patterns, and result processing workflows. Detail authentication methods, API key management, and rate limiting considerations. Include examples of search queries for different claim types, result filtering and ranking algorithms, and data extraction patterns. Document error handling for network failures, invalid queries, and empty result sets. Provide performance optimization guidelines, caching strategies, and result deduplication techniques. Include debugging approaches and monitoring tools for search API interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings View
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SettingsView component responsible for user preferences and application configuration. Document the settings interface layout, preference categories, and user interaction patterns. Detail the configuration options available to users including audio settings, notification preferences, privacy controls, and integration settings. Explain the state management for settings persistence, default value handling, and preference synchronization. Include examples of settings validation, user feedback mechanisms, and settings reset functionality. Document the integration with system settings, permission management, and background processing controls. Provide guidance on extending the settings interface with new options and maintaining consistency with iOS settings design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the FactChecking Live system. Describe the high-level design showing the modular MVVM-inspired architecture with centralized state management. Document the component relationships including the FactCheckCoordinator as the central orchestrator, service layer architecture, and UI component hierarchy. Explain the data flow from audio capture through speech recognition, claim extraction, evidence retrieval, and verdict synthesis. Detail the architectural patterns used including observer pattern for state management, service locator pattern for dependency injection, and pipeline pattern for processing stages. Include system boundaries, integration points with Apple's ecosystem (AVFoundation, ActivityKit, WidgetKit), and external API integrations. Document the separation of concerns between core services, features, and system integrations. Address cross-cutting concerns like error handling, logging, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the data flow architecture in FactChecking Live. Detail the sequential processing pipeline from audio input through the complete fact-checking workflow. Document the data transformations at each stage: audio buffers → speech recognition → transcript → claim extraction → evidence gathering → verdict synthesis. Explain the data models and their evolution through the pipeline, including Claim, Evidence, and Verdict objects. Detail the buffering and streaming mechanisms for real-time processing. Include error propagation and fallback mechanisms throughout the data flow. Address performance considerations and optimization strategies for each processing stage. Provide concrete examples of data transformation and state changes at each pipeline step.

wiki_catalogs.name: Claim Extraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the claim extraction service that uses AI-powered processing to identify factual claims from transcribed speech. Document the ClaimExtractionService implementation including Qwen API integration, claim detection algorithms, and quality assessment mechanisms. Explain the Claim data model including claim text, confidence scores, temporal positioning, and metadata. Detail the AI-powered claim identification process including natural language processing, fact extraction techniques, and filtering criteria. Document API configuration, authentication methods, and response processing. Include claim quality scoring, filtering thresholds, and error handling strategies. Provide practical examples of claim extraction workflows, API integration patterns, and integration with evidence retrieval services.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for FactChecking Live's state management architecture. Detail the observable state pattern implementation using @Observable macros, global state coordination through AppState, and reactive state updates across the application. Document the FactCheckCoordinator's role as the central state orchestrator, managing session state, coordinating between services, and broadcasting state changes. Explain state flow patterns from audio capture through speech recognition to final verdict synthesis. Cover state persistence mechanisms, state synchronization across components, and error state management. Include examples of state subscription patterns, state mutation strategies, and debugging techniques for complex state interactions. Document best practices for maintaining state consistency and performance optimization in the reactive architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Client Base Class
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the APIClient base class that provides common patterns for all API communications. Document the generic request methods, JSON request handling, and response validation mechanisms. Detail the error handling system including custom APIError enum variants, retry logic with exponential backoff, and timeout configurations. Explain the URLSession configuration, logging integration, and performance monitoring capabilities. Include examples of extending the base client for new API integrations, custom header management, and authentication token handling. Document best practices for API client usage, thread safety considerations, and memory management. Provide debugging techniques, error code references, and troubleshooting guides for common API communication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: FactCheck Session View
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the FactCheckSessionView component, the real-time fact-checking interface. Document the session management UI including live audio visualization, transcript display, claim extraction progress indicators, and verdict presentation. Explain the real-time data binding patterns for audio levels, speech recognition results, and fact-checking pipeline status. Detail the user interaction elements for session control, claim review, evidence exploration, and result sharing. Document the dynamic content updates, progress tracking, and error state handling. Include examples of responsive UI updates, accessibility features for real-time content, and performance optimization for continuous data display. Provide guidance on customizing the session interface, adding new visual indicators, and integrating additional fact-checking information displays.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core services that power the FactChecking Live system. Document the audio processing pipeline including real-time audio capture, buffer management, and quality optimization. Explain the speech recognition service implementation with on-device transcription, rolling transcript management, and partial result handling. Detail the claim extraction service using AI-powered processing with Qwen API integration, quality assessment, and filtering mechanisms. Document the evidence retrieval service for multi-source evidence gathering, provider integration strategies, and result validation. Explain the verdict synthesis service for final verdict generation, confidence scoring algorithms, and reasoning chain documentation. Include service interfaces, dependency injection patterns, error handling strategies, and performance considerations. Provide practical examples of service usage and integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Points
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the system integration points and external dependencies in FactChecking Live. Detail the integration with Apple's ecosystem including AVFoundation for audio processing, ActivityKit for Live Activities, WidgetKit for Dynamic Island widgets, and ReplayKit for broadcast extension functionality. Explain the external API integrations including Qwen API for language model processing and web search APIs. Document the app group communication between main app and broadcast extension. Detail the permission requirements and entitlement configurations. Include the integration architecture for Siri Shortcuts and App Intents. Address the security considerations and data privacy implications of each integration point. Provide configuration examples and troubleshooting guidance for integration setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Evidence Retrieval
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the evidence retrieval service that gathers supporting and contradicting evidence from multiple sources. Document the EvidenceRetrievalService implementation including multi-source evidence gathering, provider integration strategies, and result validation mechanisms. Explain the Evidence data model including source information, evidence text, confidence scores, and relevance ratings. Detail the evidence gathering pipeline including claim processing, source querying, result filtering, and cross-verification. Document integration strategies for different evidence providers, rate limiting, and caching mechanisms. Include evidence quality assessment, source credibility evaluation, and result aggregation techniques. Provide practical examples of evidence retrieval workflows, provider integration patterns, and integration with verdict synthesis services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the application structure and navigation system of FactChecking Live. Document the main application entry point, window configuration, and overall app lifecycle management. Detail the tab-based navigation architecture, scene management, and state preservation strategies. Include examples of app delegate configuration, scene delegate handling, and multi-window support if applicable. Provide practical examples of verdict synthesis workflows, confidence scoring calculations, and integration with Live Activity and UI components.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for system integration features in FactChecking Live. Provide examples of user workflows, voice command patterns, and integration with Apple's ecosystem. Address troubleshooting integration issues and performance considerations for system-level operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for FactChecking Live. Detail the core data structures including Claim model for extracted factual claims, Evidence model for supporting or contradicting information, and Verdict model for final fact-checking results with confidence scoring. Document the FactCheckSession model for session tracking and history management. Explain the Source enumeration for evidence provenance and categorization. Detail the state management architecture using the observable pattern, global state coordination through AppState, and reactive updates throughout the application. Include data validation rules, business logic constraints, and data lifecycle management. Document the relationship between different models and how they flow through the fact-checking pipeline. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the external API integrations in FactChecking Live. Document the Qwen API integration including authentication methods, request/response schemas, and API endpoints for language model processing. Detail the SearchAPI integration for web search functionality and result processing. Document the APIClient base class and common patterns for API communication, error handling strategies, and rate limiting considerations. Include authentication configuration, API key management, and security best practices. Provide concrete examples of API requests and responses, parameter specifications, and error codes. Document the retry mechanisms, timeout configurations, and performance optimization techniques. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and migration strategies. Include debugging tools and monitoring approaches for API interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components and Views
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the UI components and views in FactChecking Live. Document the main application structure including the tab-based navigation system with Home, History, and Settings views. Detail the FactCheckSessionView implementation for real-time fact-checking interface, user interaction patterns, and status display. Explain the HomeView design and functionality for primary app access. Document the SettingsView configuration options and user preferences management. Include SwiftUI component composition patterns, state binding strategies, and view lifecycle management. Detail the custom UI components like VerdictHistoryRow for displaying fact-check results, color coding for verdict types, and responsive design considerations. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the utility classes and extensions in FactChecking Live. wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation for FactChecking Live. Provide step-by-step debugging procedures for each major component including audio capture troubleshooting, speech recognition calibration, and network API errors. Include diagnostic tools and logging strategies using the Logger utility. Document performance optimization techniques for memory usage, CPU consumption, and battery life. Address platform-specific issues for different iOS versions and device capabilities. Provide solutions for broadcast extension problems, Siri Shortcuts integration issues, and widget update failures. Include frequently asked questions about app permissions, feature limitations, and usage recommendations. Document recovery procedures and fallback strategies for critical system failures.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# FactShield: Live Fact-Checking System Architecture

## Project Introduction

**FactShield** is a revolutionary real-time fact-checking system that transforms how people verify information while consuming live content. The project delivers a comprehensive solution through two primary surfaces: a browser extension (Chrome/Edge/Firefox) and an iOS app with Dynamic Island integration.

### Purpose Statement
FactShield addresses the critical information verification gap in today's digital landscape by providing instant, reliable fact-checking capabilities as users consume live content on Instagram, YouTube, Spotify, and other platforms. The system operates continuously in the background, automatically detecting and verifying factual claims without interrupting the user experience.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time verification**: Deliver fact-checking results within 10 seconds of claim detection
- **Multi-platform coverage**: Support live audio/video content across major social media and streaming platforms  
- **Transparency**: Provide full source attribution and reasoning chains for every verdict
- **Accessibility**: Offer seamless integration through Action Button shortcuts and Dynamic Island notifications
- **Accuracy**: Implement a triple-verification pipeline that surpasses existing solutions through multi-source cross-checking

### Target Audience
- **General consumers** seeking quick verification of claims during live content consumption
- **Social media users** who encounter potentially misleading information in real-time
- **Journalists and researchers** requiring rapid fact-checking capabilities
- **Educators and students** needing immediate verification of historical or current events

## Technical Architecture

FactShield implements a sophisticated distributed architecture that combines real-time audio processing, machine learning pipelines, and native mobile integration.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[User Interface Layer] --> B[Core Services Layer]
    B --> C[Audio Processing Engine]
    B --> D[Speech Recognition]
    B --> E[Machine Learning Pipeline]
    B --> F[Network Communication]
    
    C --> G[AVAudioEngine]
    C --> H[ReplayKit Extension]
    
    D --> I[SFSpeechRecognizer]
    D --> J[Whisper Integration]
    
    E --> K[Claim Extraction]
    E --> L[Evidence Retrieval]
    E --> M[Verdict Synthesis]
    
    F --> N[WebSocket Connection]
    F --> O[REST API]
    
    subgraph "iOS App"
        P[Dynamic Island]
        Q[Live Activities]
        R[Widgets]
    end
    
    subgraph "Browser Extension"
        S[Content Scripts]
        T[Side Panel UI]
        U[Offscreen Document]
    end
    
    A --> P
    A --> S
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture follows a modular design pattern with clear separation of concerns:

**Frontend Components:**
- **iOS App**: SwiftUI-based application with native iOS integrations
- **Browser Extension**: Content scripts and side panel UI for cross-platform coverage
- **Widgets**: Dynamic Island and lock screen integration for real-time status updates

**Core Services:**
- **Audio Processing**: Real-time audio capture and preprocessing
- **Speech Recognition**: On-device and cloud-based transcription services
- **Machine Learning Pipeline**: Multi-stage fact-checking with LLM orchestration
- **Network Layer**: WebSocket connections and REST API communication

**Integration Points:**
- **Apple Platforms**: ActivityKit, WidgetKit, AppIntents, ReplayKit
- **Third-party APIs**: Qwen API, Google Fact Check Tools, web search services
- **Storage Systems**: Local persistence and shared containers

### Design Patterns

The system employs several key design patterns:

**Observer Pattern**: Used extensively for state management across the application lifecycle
**Service Locator Pattern**: Centralized service discovery and dependency injection
**Pipeline Pattern**: Sequential processing stages for claim extraction, evidence retrieval, and verdict synthesis
**Factory Pattern**: Dynamic creation of audio capture modes and service instances

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Bootstrap** (`FactShield/FactShield/App/FactShieldApp.swift`)
The main application entry point initializes the SwiftUI application and sets up core state management. The app implements a tab-based navigation system with three primary sections: Home, History, and Settings.

**Global State Management** (`FactShield/FactShield/App/AppState.swift`)
Centralized state management using Swift's `@Observable` macro, providing thread-safe access to application state across all components.

### Core Modules

**Fact-Checking Coordinator** (`FactShield/FactShield/Features/FactCheck/FactCheckCoordinator.swift`)
The central orchestrator that coordinates the entire fact-checking pipeline. This component manages audio capture callbacks, schedules periodic claim extraction, and maintains the complete verification workflow.

**Audio Capture Service** (`FactShield/FactShield/Core/Audio/AudioCaptureService.swift`)
Implements real-time audio capture using AVAudioEngine with sophisticated buffer management and threading for optimal performance.

**Speech Recognition Service** (`FactShield/FactShield/Core/Speech/SpeechRecognitionService.swift`)
Provides on-device speech-to-text conversion with rolling transcript buffering and automatic restart capabilities.

**Claim Extraction Service** (`FactShield/FactShield/Core/Claims/ClaimExtractionService.swift`)
Utilizes the Qwen API to extract verifiable claims from audio transcripts using structured JSON responses and robust error handling.

### Configuration Approach

**Constants Management** (`FactShield/FactShield/Utilities/Constants.swift`)
Centralized configuration management for API endpoints, audio parameters, and feature flags. The constants system ensures consistent configuration across all components.

**Build Configuration** (`FactShield/Package.swift`)
Swift Package Manager configuration defining platform requirements (iOS 17+, macOS 14+) and library exports.

### External Dependencies

The project maintains minimal external dependencies to ensure stability and performance:

- **SwiftUI**: Native iOS UI framework for declarative interface building
- **AVFoundation**: Core audio processing and speech recognition
- **ActivityKit**: Live Activity integration for Dynamic Island
- **WidgetKit**: Widget and lock screen functionality
- **AppIntents**: Siri Shortcuts and Action Button integration

### Integration Points

**Live Activity Integration** (`FactShield/FactShield/Widgets/FactShieldLiveActivity.swift`)
Real-time status updates displayed on the Dynamic Island, providing users with immediate feedback during fact-checking operations.

**Broadcast Extension** (`FactShield/FactShield/BroadcastExtension/SampleHandler.swift`)
ReplayKit-based broadcast extension for high-fidelity audio capture from system audio sources.

**App Shortcuts** (`FactShield/FactShield/Intents/FactShieldShortcuts.swift`)
Siri Shortcuts integration enabling one-tap activation through the Action Button on supported devices.

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class FactShieldApp {
        +AppState appState
        +FactCheckCoordinator coordinator
        +ContentView contentView
    }
    
    class FactCheckCoordinator {
        +AudioCaptureService audioCapture
        +SpeechRecognitionService speechRecognizer
        +ClaimExtractionService claimExtractor
        +EvidenceRetrievalService evidenceRetriever
        +VerdictSynthesisService verdictSynthesizer
        +ActivityManager activityManager
    }
    
    class AudioCaptureService {
        +AVAudioEngine engine
        +Bool isListening
        +onAudioBuffer callback
    }
    
    class SpeechRecognitionService {
        +SFSpeechRecognizer speechRecognizer
        +String currentTranscript
        +Bool isRecognizing
    }
    
    class QwenAPI {
        +APIClient apiClient
        +String baseURL
        +chatCompletion() async
    }
    
    FactShieldApp --> FactCheckCoordinator
    FactCheckCoordinator --> AudioCaptureService
    FactCheckCoordinator --> SpeechRecognitionService
    FactCheckCoordinator --> QwenAPI
    SpeechRecognitionService --> QwenAPI
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Audio Processing**
The system captures audio from either the microphone or system audio sources, processing streams in real-time to detect and verify factual claims. The audio processing pipeline utilizes iOS's advanced audio engineering capabilities with automatic echo cancellation and noise suppression.

**Multi-Stage Verification Pipeline**
FactShield implements a sophisticated three-layer verification system:
1. **Claim Extraction**: Atomic claim identification from audio transcripts
2. **Evidence Retrieval**: Multi-source search across web, fact-check databases, and academic sources
3. **Cross-Verification**: Weighted majority voting with confidence scoring

**Dynamic Island Integration**
Unique to iOS, the system provides real-time status updates through the Dynamic Island, displaying verification progress, claim status, and final verdicts without interrupting the user's current activity.

### Implementation Highlights

**Audio Capture Modes** (`FactShield/FactShield/Core/Audio/AudioCaptureService.swift`)
The system supports two distinct audio capture modes:
- **Microphone Mode**: Uses AVAudioEngine with iOS's built-in AEC for capturing ambient audio
- **Broadcast Mode**: ReplayKit-based system audio capture for high-fidelity recordings

**Live Activity Management** (`FactShield/FactShield/Widgets/FactShieldLiveActivity.swift`)
Sophisticated status management through Apple's ActivityKit, providing real-time updates with appropriate refresh rates and user interaction capabilities.

**Error Handling and Recovery** (`FactShield/FactShield/Utilities/Logger.swift`)
Comprehensive logging and error recovery mechanisms ensuring system stability and providing detailed diagnostics for troubleshooting.

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Listening: Start Audio Capture
    Listening --> Processing: Audio Buffer Received
    Processing --> ClaimExtraction: Extract Claims
    ClaimExtraction --> EvidenceRetrieval: Retrieve Sources
    EvidenceRetrieval --> VerdictSynthesis: Cross-Check Evidence
    VerdictSynthesis --> Complete: Generate Final Verdict
    Complete --> Idle: Reset State
    
    Processing --> Error: Processing Failed
    Error --> Idle: Error Recovery
    
    Listening --> Stopped: Stop Audio Capture
    Stopped --> Processing: Submit Transcript
```

**Real-time Status Updates**
The system provides granular status updates throughout the verification process, with Dynamic Island displaying appropriate icons and text based on the current operation phase.

**Multi-Platform Support**
Seamless integration across iOS devices, macOS systems, and web browsers through platform-specific optimizations and unified backend services.

**Privacy and Security**
All audio processing occurs locally on device with optional cloud processing for enhanced accuracy. User data is handled with strict privacy controls and minimal data retention policies.

---

</blog>
